'''Terelian ore''', also known as '''Terilian ore''', was a very dense, valuable [[Ore/Legends|ore]] that at one point was [[Mining/Legends|mined]] on an [[Unidentified Trebodar system asteroid|asteroid]] in the [[Trebodar system]]. The ore was refined at a facility on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]], after which special large ore [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]] picked it up at the mine's [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]].<ref name="Shadis" />

At some point, the asteroid mine was abandoned, with a group of [[Galderian/Legends|Galderian]] [[Galderian Slave Traders|enslavers]] setting up a base there by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. By that time, multiple [[Limb/Legends|fist]]-sized balls of Terelian ore—which in a specific market could be sold for up to fifty thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]—had been sealed in the mine's vault room, with the Galderians being convinced that the room contained something highly valuable yet not being able to breach its door through the use of [[Blaster/Legends|heavy blasters]].<ref name="Shadis" />

==Behind the scenes==
Terelian ore was mentioned in "[[Strange Bedfellows]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[John Zinser]] and published in the [[July]]–[[August]] [[1994]] [[SHADIS Magazine 14|fourteenth issue]] of the [[SHADIS Magazine|''Shadis'']] magazine for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Since that adventure was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Shadis" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>
